checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=30,005,000 (2013-12-31) declining to 25,941,000 (2014-12-31) while Revenues=152,550,000 (2013 annual) grew to 182,644,000 (2014 annual)
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2015-11-06, balance sheet comparative data / 10-K FY2013 & FY2014 Revenues
10-K FY2013 balance sheet and income statement

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=82,644,000 (2011-12-31) declined to 65,815,000 (2012-12-31), consistent with a large impairment rather than growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 income statement and cash flow statement

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-19,090,000 (2014 annual) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=22,419,000 (2014 annual); OperatingIncomeLoss=4,962,000 (2014 annual) also positive, unlike net loss
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 income statement and cash flow statement

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-21,496,000 (Q3 2014) while OperatingIncomeLoss=193,000 (Q3 2014), indicating a large non-operating charge driving the quarterly loss

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin computed as (Revenues-CostOfServices)/Revenues declined gradually: 2011 45.6% (Revenues=138,726,000, CostOfServices=75,433,000) to 2014 37.3% (Revenues=182,644,000, CostOfServices=114,581,000)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss swings from -21,496,000 (Q3 2014) to 20,868,000 (Q2 2015) while Revenues moved only from 47,238,000 (Q3 2014) to 35,346,000 (Q2 2015)
evidence.location: 10-Q Q2 2015 and comparative Q3 2014 income statement

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=15,922,000 (2013-12-31) declined to 13,766,000 (2014-12-31) while CostOfServices grew from 91,858,000 (2013) to 114,581,000 (2014)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013/2014 balance sheet and income statement; 10-Q 2015-11-06 comparative balance sheet

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent grew only from 577,000 (2012-12-31) to 621,000 (2013-12-31), +7.6%, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 25,988,000 to 30,005,000, +15.5%
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 notes - allowance for doubtful accounts

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 8-K filed 2013-12-16 (accession 0001193125-13-474701) provides recast historical balance sheet and income statement data for periods as far back as 2009-2010 (e.g., AccountsPayableCurrent=12,896,000 for 2011-12-31, Revenues=85,828,000 for 2010 annual), outside the normal 10-K/10-Q cycle
10-K filings were made annually with no NT 10-K, 10-K/A, or 10-Q/A restatement filings observed in the chronology (e.g., 10-K filed 2015-03-10 for FY2014, 10-K filed 2014-03-03 for FY2013)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Annual and quarterly data available for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, Assets, Liabilities, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Goodwill, and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent spanning fiscal years 2009 through Q3 2015

top_signals: Persistent, large divergence between NetIncomeLoss and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities in 2012 and 2014 (e.g., NetIncomeLoss=-35,196,000 vs OCF=19,901,000 in 2012; NetIncomeLoss=-19,090,000 vs OCF=22,419,000 in 2014), driven by large non-operating/non-cash items (goodwill impairment, likely tax valuation allowance charges) rather than core operations
Accounts payable declined (15,922,000 to 13,766,000, FY2013-FY2014) while cost of services rose materially (91,858,000 to 114,581,000), a mild divergence worth monitoring for reserve/accrual timing

An 8-K filed 2013-12-16 recast multiple years of historical balance sheet and income-statement data outside the normal 10-K/10-Q amendment process, an atypical (though not necessarily improper) filing pattern

Extreme earnings volatility (quarterly swings from -21,496,000 to +20,868,000 net income) tied to one-time items (impairment, divestiture gains, tax items) rather than smoothing, reducing rather than increasing manipulation concern on the smoothing dimension but adding interpretive complexity to core earnings quality
